adas.name: Camera suite (forward + surround)

items.t: Forward mono → stereo camera: delete standalone radar on SAE L2+
items.save: Delete 1× radar (£34-68), depth perception without radar
items.bench: Tesla Vision (2021): deleted all radar from Model 3/Y; Mobileye SuperVision (2023): stereo
items.note: Forward radar is £34–68 BOM. Deleting it with camera-only depth sensing removes a sensor, its housing, connector and harness — and simplifies the perception fusion stack.

items.t: Surround cameras on shared image-processor SoC (delete dedicated surround ECU)
items.bench: Mobileye EyeQ5H surroundview on shared ADAS SoC (2023); Continental MFC6 multi-function ca
items.note: A dedicated surround-view ECU (£26–51) does only one job. Moving it to the ADAS SoC costs incremental compute but deletes an entire ECU housing and assembly.

items.t: Camera-based rain and light sensing — delete standalone sensors
items.save: Delete rain sensor module (£6.8-13) + light sensor (£2.5-5.1)
items.bench: Bosch multi-purpose camera (rain+lane+ADAS on one lens, 2019); Valeo SCALA rain-sensing ca
items.note: Standalone rain and light sensors are small-cost but nonzero BOM items. Deletion via camera software is pure cost reduction with no new hardware.

items.t: DMS camera on shared forward ADAS SoC (Euro NCAP 2024 mandate)
items.bench: Mobileye EyeQ5H DMS on shared SoC (Zeekr 001, 2023); Continental ContiGuard DMS on ADAS So
items.note: Euro NCAP mandate means every new model needs DMS. A standalone ECU at £26–51 is avoidable cost when the ADAS SoC already has capacity. Deletion saves BOM on every vehicle sold.

items.t: Camera-first Tesla Vision strategy — delete forward radar entirely
items.bench: Tesla Vision across Model 3/Y/S/X/Cybertruck from 2021; Mobileye EyeQ6L camera-centric L2+
items.note: At scale, camera-first deletes the single most expensive active sensor on the car for non-premium L2 vehicles. Combined with Mobileye or Nvidia's neural-depth estimation, camera-only meets regulatory

adas.baseline: Dedicated forward mono camera (Mobileye EyeQ4), 4× surround cameras on separate image-processor SoC, standalone rain sensor, standalone light sensor, separate DMS camera with own ECU. Total: 7 cameras, 4 ECUs, 4 harness
adas.fn: Provide forward lane / object detection for L2 ADAS, surround-view parking assistance, driver monitoring for drowsiness and distraction, plus rain and ambient-light sensing for automatic wiper/light activation.

adas.name: Radar & LiDAR suite

items.t: 77 GHz corner radar multi-function (L2 ADAS + blind-spot + cross-traffic alert)
items.save: Delete standalone BSM radar (£17-30/corner), 2 ECU stubs
items.bench: Bosch mid-range radar MRR Gen3 (2023) multi-function; Continental ARS540 4D corner multi-f
items.note: Standalone BSM radars (£17–30 each) are added to the rear quarters for NCAP-mandated blind-spot detection. A multi-function corner radar achieves all three functions without added hardware.

items.t: LiDAR right-size: reduce scan rate and FOV for highway vs urban use case
items.bench: Luminar Iris highway-optimised LiDAR (250 m range, 120° H × 20° V, on Volvo EX90 2024); In
items.note: LiDAR is £170–680+ per unit depending on specification. Right-sizing FOV, range and scan rate to the actual use-case eliminates over-specification — the largest single LiDAR cost lever.

items.t: 4D imaging radar replacing standalone LiDAR on L2+ vehicles
items.bench: Continental ARS540 4D imaging radar (2023); Arbe Phoenix 4D radar (Stellantis partnership
items.note: Scanning LiDAR at £170–680 per unit is the largest single ADAS BOM item for non-robotaxi vehicles. 4D imaging radar at £68–128 delivers comparable object detection for highway L2+ at 70–80% cost reduc

items.t: Solid-state LiDAR (MEMS / OPA) vs spinning mechanical scanner
items.save: Unit cost −50-70% vs spinning LiDAR, reliability ↑
items.bench: Luminar Iris (MEMS galvo, Volvo EX90 2024 production); Innoviz Two solid-state (BMW L3 202
items.note: Spinning LiDAR requires a motor, slip ring, IP67 rotating seal and large roofline package. Solid-state replaces all of this with a stamped housing. Cost trajectory is toward camera parity by 2027 acco

adas.baseline: One forward long-range radar (77 GHz), two front-corner radars, two rear-corner radars, one roof-mount mechanical spinning LiDAR (64-beam) for L3 models; separate ADAS ECU per sensor domain.
adas.fn: Provide all-weather range measurement, velocity (Doppler), blind-spot and cross-traffic detection, and high-density point-cloud mapping for automated driving functions.

adas.name: Airbag system

items.t: Airbag count right-size by NCAP test matrix — delete knee airbag on high-sill BEV
items.save: Delete knee airbag module (£15-30 per side)
items.bench: Tesla Model 3/Y: no knee airbag (NCAP 5-star, 2021–2023); BYD Seal: no knee airbag; Rivian
items.note: Knee airbag deletion removes module, inflator, squib, connector, wiring stub and instrument panel integration feature. The BEV platform justification makes this a credible deletion with existing NCAP

items.t: Centralised ACU replacing distributed satellite sensor architecture
items.save: Delete 6–8 satellite sensors (£3.4-6.8 each), simplify wiring
items.bench: Bosch SDM centralised ACU with MEMS accelerometers (mainstream since 2018); Continental MR
items.note: Each satellite sensor is £3.4–6.8 BOM. Deleting 8 saves £27–54 per vehicle. Central MEMS achieves equivalent crash discrimination with higher MEMS sensitivity at lower system cost.

items.t: Far-side airbag via seat-mounted centre airbag (delete dedicated far-side module)
items.save: Delete far-side airbag module (£21-38), simplify far-side wiring
items.bench: Mercedes-Benz S-Class (2021): centre airbag in driver seat cushion for far-side protection
items.note: Euro NCAP far-side mandatory from 2023 (AOP protocol). A dedicated far-side airbag on each side doubles the module count for far-side protection. A single centre bag provides the same function for bot

items.t: Curtain airbag L/R tooling commonisation (mirror-image strategy)
items.bench: Autoliv curtain airbag symmetric design across BMW G-series; ZF TRW symmetric curtain on S
items.note: Curtain airbag tooling NRE is significant (£0.8–2.55M per programme across L+R). Commonising L/R also consolidates volume on one SKU for better piece-price leverage with Autoliv/ZF TRW/Joyson.

items.t: Airbag inflator right-size — single-stage vs dual-stage gas generator
items.bench: Autoliv micro-gas-generator (MGG) right-sizing; ZF TRW inflator family optimisation; ARC A
items.note: Inflator is the highest unit-cost component within each airbag module (typically 40–55% of module BOM). Right-sizing from dual-stage to single-stage on non-mandated positions removes cost without perf

adas.baseline: Driver airbag, front passenger airbag, 2× side thorax airbags, 2× curtain airbags, 2× knee airbags, far-side airbag, 1× central airbag; distributed ACU architecture with 8–12 satellite sensors; separate inflator per bag.
adas.fn: Deploy restraint bags in a crash event to limit occupant head, chest and knee loads within Euro NCAP and FMVSS 208 injury criteria — timed precisely to crash pulse, seat position and occupant classification.

adas.name: Seatbelt system

items.t: Pretensioner pyrotechnic right-size: 1-stage vs 2-stage gas generator
items.bench: Autoliv 1-stage pretensioner on rear outboard positions across Toyota/Lexus (2021); ZF TRW
items.note: Pretensioner gas generator is 30–45% of seatbelt module BOM. Right-sizing eliminates unnecessary dual-stage cost on positions where single-stage meets all regulatory and NCAP requirements.

items.t: Load limiter integration in spool — delete external torsion bar assembly
items.save: Delete external load-limiter assembly (£3.4-7.6), part count −3
items.bench: Autoliv inertia-reel integrated load limiter across VW MQB platform (2019); Continental Sa
items.note: An external load-limiter assembly adds 3+ parts and a separate bracket to each seating position. Integration into the spool is a mature, proven approach that removes these at no performance penalty.

items.t: Common seatbelt retractor across front and rear seat positions
items.bench: Autoliv common retractor platform (CPRS) across Ford C2 platform (Kuga, Galaxy, S-Max 2020
items.note: A typical 5-seat vehicle carries 5 unique retractor part numbers. Commonising to 1–2 families halves or quarters the SKU count, concentrates volume and allows a simpler supplier base.

items.t: Delete belt-in-seat (BIS) on non-performance rear variants
items.save: Delete BIS system (£38-77 per seating position)
items.bench: Porsche Taycan Sport Turismo BIS on rear (performance variant only); BMW 4-series Gran Cou
items.note: BIS adds significant cost per rear outboard position — £38–77 for the additional hardware integration vs a B-pillar retractor. On non-sport variants with a conventional bench seat, BIS provides no mea

items.t: Buckle sensor standardise — one part number across all positions
items.bench: Ford common buckle sensor on C2 platform (2020); Hyundai/Kia common hall-effect buckle sen
items.note: Five seating positions with unique buckle sensors (different part numbers due to legacy design) multiplies qualification cost, inventory and supplier diversity. A single common sensor is a straightfor

adas.baseline: Separate pyrotechnic pretensioner per seating position, 2-stage load limiter in spool, 5 unique retractor assemblies per vehicle, separate buckle sensor per position, belt-in-seat on rear-row performance variants.
adas.fn: Restrain occupant during a crash by tensioning and energy-limiting the belt webbing in precise coordination with airbag deployment — minimising chest deflection, neck load and submarining across all NCAP and FMVSS test s

battery.name: Battery Cells

items.t: NMC → LFP chemistry for range-appropriate models
items.bench: CATL (BYD Blade, Tesla LFP Standard)
items.note: LFP: lower cobalt/nickel cost, longer cycle life (3000+), safer thermal. Penalty: 15-20% energy density

items.t: CATL M3P (Mn-doped LFP): density bridge
items.bench: CATL Shenxing Plus, BYD Sea-Lion 6
items.note: M3P achieves 210 Wh/kg at LFP-comparable cost — narrows density gap to NMC while keeping cost advantage

items.t: 4680 cylindrical: 15-25% $/kWh reduction vs 21700
items.bench: Tesla Giga Texas/Berlin 4680
items.note: Tabless electrode: less cell parts, higher energy density, faster charge. At scale ($B capex) breaks NMC21700 cost floor

items.t: Localise cell supply (China → regional)
items.bench: CATL Germany, SK On US/Hungary
items.note: EU/US: eliminate 18% import duty + 3-5% logistics cost. Regional cell supply also qualifies for IRA/EU CRMA subsidies

items.t: SoC window extension via better BMS algorithm
items.bench: Tesla OTA improvements, BYD
items.note: Narrow DoD buffer from 10-90% → 8-92% via improved SoC estimation: more usable energy from same cells

battery.baseline: NMC622/811 pouch or prismatic cells at pack level. 400V architecture. Sourced from LG/CATL/Samsung SDI.

battery.name: Module Architecture

items.t: Cell-to-Pack (CTP): delete module frame entirely
items.bench: CATL Qilin, BYD Blade, Tesla 4680
items.note: Delete module housing, reduce busbars. Thermal management direct on cells. Best-in-class: CATL Qilin 255 Wh/kg

items.t: Reduce module SKU count: 6→2 per pack
items.bench: BMW iX 3.0 platform
items.note: Common module height/width × 2 lengths covers all pack capacities: shared tooling, volume on single process line

items.t: Ultrasonic busbar welding (replace bolt-on)
items.bench: Manz, Fronius ultrasonic welding
items.note: Welded Al busbars: lower resistance, no torque creep, 30% faster vs bolted tab assembly

items.t: Adhesive bonding module-to-tray (replace fasteners)
items.bench: BMW i4, Audi Q8 e-tron
items.note: Structural adhesive module bonding: vibration-optimised, eliminates 20+ fasteners per module

items.t: Delete inter-module HV connector (direct busbar routing)
items.bench: Tesla Model 3, BYD Blade
items.note: CTP/CTC direct cell-to-busbar eliminates inter-module connectors — requires pack-level serviceability planning

battery.baseline: Module-based pack: 4-8 modules per pack. Individual module frame + busbar + housing.

battery.name: Battery Management System

items.t: Centralised BMS (one node vs distributed per module)
items.bench: CATL centralised BMS, VW Group
items.note: Single BMS master with ASIC cell monitoring ICs: fewer PCBs, one calibration, simpler harness topology

items.t: Wireless BMS (wBMS): delete cell monitoring harness
items.bench: GM Ultium (Analog Devices wBMS)
items.note: Proven in production: GM Hummer EV/Lyriq. Eliminates harness looms between modules. Pack reassembly simplified

items.t: Integrate BMS function into VCU/Domain controller
items.bench: Rivian R1T/R1S, Lucid Air
items.note: High-performance compute node absorbs BMS: one fewer ECU, shared HW platform, one validation effort

items.t: Model-based SoC/SoH (no additional hardware)
items.bench: Tesla OTA SW updates, NIO
items.note: Improved Kalman/EKF algorithm for SoC: tighter DoD buffers possible → more usable capacity from same cell

items.t: Pack-level isolation monitoring (vs per-module)
items.bench: CATL, Hitachi standard practice
items.note: Single pack-level IMD vs individual module IMD: simplifies topology, meets IEC 60664 at pack boundary

battery.baseline: Distributed BMS: standalone BMS master ECU + cell monitoring boards per module. Wired daisy-chain monitoring.

battery.name: Thermal Management

items.t: Ribbon-fin Al extrusion cooling plate (vs brazed flat)
items.bench: Modine, Dana Thermal standard
items.note: Extruded ribbon-fin plate: fewer brazing ops, better geometry consistency, faster lead-time vs complex brazed plate

items.t: Reduce TIM thickness via surface flatness spec
items.bench: Henkel, Shin-Etsu TIM optimisation
items.note: Specify cell bottom/cooling plate flatness to enable ≤0.4mm TIM vs current 0.6-0.8mm — same thermal resistance

items.t: Single-fluid (ATF) cooling: motor + battery loop
items.bench: Ford F-150 Lightning, Lucid Air
items.note: One pump, one fluid, fewer heat exchangers. Ford F-150 Lightning pioneered this. Requires ATF-compatible cell selection

items.t: Delete chiller for PHEV (phase-change TIM substitution)
items.bench: Stellantis PHEV platforms
items.note: PHEV partial-charge cycles: phase-change TIM buffers heat spikes, simplifies cooling loop vs full BEV chiller

items.t: Direct refrigerant cooling (DRC) delete chiller
items.bench: BMW i4 M50, Porsche Taycan Turbo
items.note: Refrigerant flows directly through battery floor plate: eliminates glycol/water chiller, fewer HXs — requires R744 or R1234yf rated sealing

battery.baseline: Bottom cooling plate (brazed Al ribbons). Separate chiller and HVAC loop. Liquid glycol circuit.

battery.name: Pack Enclosure & Structure

items.t: Al HPDC integrated tray (vs welded extrusion frame)
items.bench: BMW i4/iX, Hyundai Ioniq 5
items.note: Cast Al tray integrates cross-members, mounting bosses, coolant ports: fewer machining ops, better sealing

items.t: GF-PP composite top cover (vs Al sheet)
items.bench: CATL standard, Mubea composites
items.note: GF-PP injection-moulded cover: 40% lighter, no corrosion, lower tooling cost than Al stampings, better thermal

items.t: Structural pack integration (CTB): BIW floor deletion
items.bench: BYD Ocean e-Platform, Tesla Model Y, NIO
items.note: Pack tray carries torsional loads → delete vehicle floor crossmembers. 10-15% total vehicle cost saving when done at platform level

items.t: Foam structural fill of pack void spaces
items.bench: Sika FoamCore, Dow structural foam
items.note: Expandable foam between cells/modules: stiffens pack, absorbs vibration, no added mass. Thermal properties manageable

items.t: Relax coolant port machining: IT6→IT7 on non-sealing faces
items.bench: Automotive machining benchmarks
items.note: Only seal-face features require IT6 — remaining faces run at IT7: reduces cycle time and tool wear

battery.baseline: Al extrusion frame + bottom Al sheet + top Al cover. Multiple internal crossmembers.

battery.name: HV Electrical System

items.t: Al busbar vs Cu at 800V (current halved)
items.bench: Porsche Taycan, Hyundai E-GMP, Lucid
items.note: At 800V, phase current halves → Al conductor viable. £/kg Al vs Cu: 80% lower. Crimp/weld terminations at joints

items.t: Pre-assembled HV harness module (kitted assembly)
items.bench: Aptiv, TE Connectivity modular HV
items.note: Pre-wired HV harness with connectors, tested before fitment: eliminate in-station connection errors, reduce assembly time

items.t: Integrate BDU + pyro fuse + current sensor: single housing
items.bench: BYD 8-in-1, Marelli BDU
items.note: Integrated BDU: isolator + fuse + current measurement in one box — delete external housing, brackets, 3 connectors

items.t: CTP: reduce HV connectors (direct cell-to-busbar)
items.bench: Tesla Model 3, BYD Blade CTP
items.note: CTP eliminates inter-module connections — busbar routes directly over cells. Fewer HVIL loops required

items.t: Standardise HV connector interface across platform
items.bench: VW Group, HVIL standard (NACS/CCS)
items.note: Common HV connector family (2-pin/3-pin) across 3-4 models: volume pricing, single qualification

battery.baseline: Cu busbars. Separate BDU housing. Manual service disconnect (MSD). Multiple HV connectors.

biw.name: Front Crash Structure

items.t: Roll-formed AHSS DP1200 bumper beam
items.bench: Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4
items.note: Replaces stamped + cast bracket assembly, 1 part vs 4, same or better ODB performance

items.t: Al extrusion crash can (symmetric L/R)
items.bench: BMW 5 Series, Audi A6
items.note: At 800K+ units/yr cost-neutral to steel, 40% lighter. L/R symmetry halves tooling cost

items.t: Tailor-welded blank (TWB) front rail
items.bench: VW MQB A0, Hyundai K3
items.note: Eliminates inner reinforcement pressing via graded TWB — single part replaces 2

items.t: PHS (22MnB5) roll-formed crash box
items.bench: BMW 5 Series Gen7
items.note: Higher intrusion resistance vs DP980 stamped, thinner gauge possible

items.t: Commonise crash can geometry L/R
items.bench: Ford Puma, Volkswagen
items.note: Single symmetric design — halves tooling investment and call-off SKU count

biw.baseline: Stamped AHSS rails + separate crash cans + roll-formed bumper beam

biw.name: A/B/C-Pillar Structure

items.t: Hot-stamped roll-formed B-pillar (1200-1500 MPa)
items.bench: VW Golf Mk8, Hyundai Ioniq 6
items.note: Delete 2-part hat-section assembly → single hot-formed part. Tool + weld ops reduced

items.t: Tailor-rolled blank (TRB) B-pillar
items.bench: BMW 3 Series G20
items.note: Variable thickness in 1 part without weld lines — delete inner reinforcement section

items.t: TWB A-pillar inner: delete inner reinforcement
items.bench: Hyundai Tucson, Ford Focus
items.note: Graded TWB provides strong base/thin top — eliminates separate reinforcement pressing

items.t: Grade right-size C/D pillar (TRIP vs PHS)
items.bench: JLR I-Pace, Skoda Octavia
items.note: C-pillar carries lower crash load — TRIP/TWIP viable, 10-18% cost saving vs over-specified PHS

items.t: Common PHS grade across A/B/C pillars
items.bench: Toyota TNGA-K
items.note: Single PHS grade: 1 HTS die pool, 1 material call-off, higher annual volume on same press

biw.baseline: B-pillar: 2-part stamped DP780+DP980 hat sections. A-pillar: TWB inner + outer pressing.

biw.name: Sill / Rocker & Floor Pan

items.t: Roll-formed closed-section sill (DP1200/UHSS)
items.bench: BMW G30, Volvo S90
items.note: Single roll-formed closed section replaces inner+outer+reinforcement, eliminates 250+ spot welds

items.t: Al extrusion sill on BEV (FMVSS214 / NCAP side-pole)
items.bench: Tesla Model Y, BMW iX
items.note: Extrusion doubles as battery side-protection rail, BEV-specific architecture

items.t: Stamp floor + tunnel from single TWB
items.bench: Toyota TNGA-GA, Suzuki Swift
items.note: Single tailor-welded pressing combining front floor + tunnel — deletes junction weld flange

items.t: Gauge right-size outer floor panels (non-loaded)
items.bench: Renault Clio, VW Polo
items.note: Topology shows 0.65mm sufficient for acoustic panels — remove 15-20% over-spec gauge

items.t: Commonise floor crossmember section across platform
items.bench: Stellantis STLA Medium
items.note: Common C-channel crossmember section across 3-4 models: shared roll-tool, volume pricing

biw.baseline: Sill: 3-part stamped hat-section assembly. Floor: separate front/rear/tunnel panels.

biw.name: Doors / Hood / Tailgate

items.t: Al outer door skin (all 4 doors)
items.bench: BMW 3 Series, Jaguar XE, Audi A4
items.note: 2.8→1.1 kg/door. Pedestrian protection improvement. Cost neutral >150K/yr. Requires hem/adhesive bond

items.t: Single-part hemmed door panel (adhesive+RSW)
items.bench: Ford Fiesta, VW Polo
items.note: Weld-bonded hem eliminates separate inner flange reinforcement and reduces sealer length

items.t: Al hood outer + Al inner frame
items.bench: Toyota Land Cruiser 300, Range Rover L460
items.note: Essential for Euro NCAP 2024 pedestrian head-impact — enables thinner bonnet cross-section

items.t: Composite liftgate (SMC outer + GMT inner)
items.bench: BMW X5 G05, Land Rover Defender L663
items.note: No corrosion, 40% lighter, enables powered tailgate at lower actuator cost

items.t: Standardise door hinge across platform (common geometry)
items.bench: Stellantis STLA Medium
items.note: Common hinge bolt pattern and pivot geometry — shared tooling, volume pricing with Tier-2

biw.baseline: Steel door skins 0.75mm CQ. Steel hood with inner frame. Steel liftgate with reinforcement.

biw.name: Roof Panel & Bows

items.t: Reduce roof bow count: 3→2 with optimised cross-section
items.bench: Mazda CX-60, Kia EV6
items.note: Topology optimisation shows 2 bows sufficient with structural adhesive bonding of roof skin

items.t: Panoramic glass structural bonding (delete reinforcement bows)
items.bench: BMW iX, Mercedes EQS
items.note: Glass-bonded panoramic surround acts as structural element — careful NCAP roof-crush validation required

items.t: Al roof outer skin (weight-budget BEV)
items.bench: BMW i7, Audi Q8 e-tron
items.note: Lowers CG, reduces total BEV weight. Cost premium £15-26 offset by battery savings on range model

items.t: CFRP fixed roof panel (performance)
items.bench: BMW M4, Porsche 718
items.note: Lowers CG for performance, premium segment. Delete bows entirely with monocoque CFRP roof

items.t: Grade right-size: HSLA220 → mild DC04 outer roof
items.bench: VW Group (general)
items.note: Roof outer not a structural load path — 0.65mm DC04 sufficient for Class A surface quality

biw.baseline: Mild steel roof outer (0.7mm). 3 pressed steel roof bows. Steel roof rails.

biw.name: Rear End Structure

items.t: Hot-stamped rear floor (1 pressing vs 3 panels)
items.bench: BMW G30 5 Series, Audi A4 B9
items.note: Single hot-formed floor pan eliminates intermediate spot welds and assembly stages

items.t: Al rear bumper beam extrusion
items.bench: Audi Q5, BMW X3 G01
items.note: Al extrusion meets RCAR/IIHS low-speed impact at lower mass. Crash cans integral or bolted

items.t: Delete spare wheel recess on BEV (flat load floor)
items.bench: Tesla Model 3/Y, Hyundai Ioniq 5
items.note: BEV flat floor enables deletion of spare tyre recess — carry compressed tyre repair kit instead

items.t: Al HPDC rear subframe (>80K/yr)
items.bench: BMW G01 X3, Mercedes W213
items.note: Al cast cradle at high volume: similar cost to welded steel, 30% lighter, better dimensional accuracy

items.t: Commonise rear wheelhouse inner (L/R symmetric)
items.bench: Renault Megane E-Tech
items.note: Symmetric inner arch pressing — one die set for both L and R, reduces BIW tooling investment

biw.baseline: 3-part stamped rear floor. Steel rear rails. Steel rear bumper beam.

biw.name: Reinforcements & NVH

items.t: Structural foam cavity fill (Sika/Dow): replace 3 discrete reinforcements
items.bench: BMW G11 7 Series, Land Rover
items.note: Pillar/sill foam fill delivers NVH + stiffness target, deletes 3 separate steel bracketing

items.t: Optimise acoustic pad area by 20-25% via position mapping
items.bench: Nissan internal process
items.note: OEM acoustic data shows 80% benefit from 60% of pad area — remove low-ROI peripheral pads

items.t: Flow-drill screws replace T-nuts in floor attachment
items.bench: VW MQB platform
items.note: FDS eliminates nut insertion op and T-nut tool investment — equivalent clamping load

items.t: Delete crash sensor bracket: integrate into subframe casting boss
items.bench: BMW F20, Porsche 992
items.note: Cast sensor boss in Al subframe or strut tower — eliminates stamped bracket + 4 weld nuts

items.t: Weld bonding (adhesive + spot weld): reduce seam sealer length 25%
items.bench: Volvo, BMW (Henkel Teroson)
items.note: Structural adhesive between flanges provides NVH benefit, allows sealer length reduction

biw.baseline: Multiple steel reinforcement stampings. Bitumen acoustic pads. Seam sealers around full perimeter.

chassis.name: Front Suspension System

items.t: Al HPDC front knuckle vs cast iron/steel
items.bench: BMW G20/G30, Audi B9 A4
items.note: Weight saving improves ride; cost premium offset at C-segment volume. ABS tone ring integration standard

items.t: Forged Al lower control arm (LCA)
items.bench: Mercedes W213, Porsche Macan
items.note: Forged Al vs stamped AHSS: 40% lighter, better crash fold, integral ball joint housing

items.t: McPherson → double-wishbone base variant delete upper arm
items.bench: Ford Focus ST, Peugeot 508
items.note: On non-performance/non-SUV: delete upper A-arm, use standard McPherson — cost advantage outweighs dynamic

items.t: Hollow stabiliser bar (12mm wall vs solid)
items.bench: Continental, ZF (OEM standard)
items.note: Same NVH/roll stiffness. Standard on BMW/Audi since 2015. Drop-in replacement for solid bar

items.t: Standardise bushing compound across platform family
items.bench: VW Group MQB/MLB
items.note: Common rubber compound shore hardness: single Tier-2 compound, volume pricing across 4+ models

chassis.baseline: Double-wishbone with cast iron/steel arms. MacPherson strut on B/C-segment. Steel coil spring + twin-tube damper.

chassis.name: Rear Suspension System

items.t: Multi-link → CTBA (twist beam) for B/C-segment
items.bench: VW Polo Mk6, Renault Clio V
items.note: CTBA adequate for FWD sub-C. Saves lateral links + knuckle + subframe vs multi-link. NVH trade-off managed with bushing tuning

items.t: Al multi-link rear arms (forged/HPDC)
items.bench: BMW 5 Series G30, Audi A6 C8
items.note: Essential for BEV unsprung mass targets. Cost-neutral vs steel at >80K/yr. Better corrosion resistance

items.t: Delete adaptive (CDC) damping on base/mid trim
items.bench: BMW 5 Series base, Audi A4 base
items.note: CDC only on Sport/M-Sport/S-Line trims — deletes actuator + ECU. Passive damper well-tuned suffices

items.t: Air spring → coil spring delete (non-load-levelling)
items.bench: Land Rover Freelander vs Discovery
items.note: Remove air spring on trim grades not requiring height adjust. Significant cost save; tuned coil spring ride parity possible

items.t: Delete rear-wheel steering (non-performance models)
items.bench: BMW 7 Series base vs M760Li
items.note: RWS only justified on L-WB or performance — delete on standard WB variants. Electric actuator cost reduces as volume grows

chassis.baseline: Multi-link with steel lateral links + trailing arm. CDC adaptive damping on mid/premium. Air spring optional.

chassis.name: Steering System

items.t: EPS motor right-size to vehicle class
items.bench: ZF, Jtekt, JTEKTSE product lines
items.note: B-segment vehicles often fitted C-segment motor spec — right-size to actual steering effort and frequency

items.t: Standardise EPS rack across 2 platform families
items.bench: VW MQB A0/A-shared rack
items.note: Common rack geometry on shared platforms: volume pricing with supplier, single homologation cycle

items.t: Delete 4-way electric column adjustment on base trim
items.bench: Audi A3 base vs Sportback
items.note: Manual reach/rake or 2-way electric sufficient for base — delete 2-axis motor + memory function

items.t: Integrate EPS ECU into vehicle domain controller
items.bench: Rivian R1T/R1S, BMW Neue Klasse
items.note: Centralised domain computing absorbs EPS function — delete standalone EPS ECU unit

chassis.baseline: Rack-assist EPAS (REPS) on C-segment+. Column-assist (CEPS) on B-segment. Variable ratio rack standard.

items.bench: Skoda Octavia, SEAT Leon
items.note: EURO 7 brake dust filter to replace dust shield function — delete shield if filter integrated into caliper

chassis.baseline: Front: 330-360mm vented disc, 4-pot fixed caliper on performance / 1-pot floating on base. Rear: 300-320mm disc + EPB.

chassis.name: Front/Rear Subframe

items.t: Al HPDC front subframe (>80K/yr)
items.bench: BMW G20 3 Series, Mercedes C-Class

items.bench: Ford Mondeo, Volvo S60

items.bench: Stellantis STLA / VW MQB

items.bench: Tesla Model 3 Highland, NIO
items.note: Large HPDC integrates front subframe + strut towers + rails in one casting — $50-100M press required

chassis.baseline: Welded steel tubular subframe. Front: hydroformed or stamped sections. Rear: welded steel cradle.

chassis.name: Wheel Hub & Bearing

items.t: HBU right-size to actual load (remove overspec)
items.bench: Schaeffler/SKF product optimisation
items.note: B/C-segment SUV often fitted D-segment HBU spec — right-size by FEA load analysis, save 10-15%

items.bench: NSK, Jtekt Gen-3 HBU

items.t: Common wheel bolt pattern across platform
items.bench: VW Group 5×112 standard
items.note: PCD commonisation: same wheel/hub tooling across D/E-segment, share wheel design, volume wheel pricing

chassis.baseline: Generation-3 hub bearing unit (HBU3). Cast iron hub. Steel ARB drop links.

edu.name: Stator winding

items.t: Square flat-wire, 8-layer hairpin (~97.9% motor efficiency)
items.bench: XPeng G6 3rd-gen PMSM
items.note: Higher fill means less copper and a shorter stack for equal torque; efficiency gain lets the battery shrink.

items.t: W-pin (continuous-wave) winding + motor downsizing
items.bench: NIO ET9 925V (79 kg, 4.3 kW/kg)
items.note: Higher power density removes active copper, steel and magnet per kW and shrinks the housing.

items.t: Round-wire → hairpin / bar winding
items.bench: VW APP550, BMW Gen5, Hyundai E-GMP, Ford F-150 Lightning
items.note: Copper is typically the #2 motor cost; lifting fill cuts conductor mass 10-20%.

items.t: Conductor section / parallel-path right-size for 800V
items.bench: Universal lever
items.note: Removes copper bought for worst-case current that the 800V duty never draws.

items.t: End-winding length reduction
items.bench: Compact-motor practice
items.note: End-turn copper is dead weight and loss; trimming it cuts material and I²R directly.

edu.baseline: Round-wire distributed winding, ~40-45% slot fill, copper conductors, varnish-impregnated, water-jacket cooled.
edu.fn: Carry phase current at low loss, deliver required MMF/torque, withstand thermal/dielectric/AC-loss stress, be wound and terminated at volume.

edu.name: Lamination core / electrical steel

items.t: Raise base/max speed (20-30k rpm) to shrink active material
items.bench: BYD ~23k, Yangwang U9 30k, Xiaomi V8s 27.2k
items.note: Active material scales with torque/size — raising speed is the most direct route to less material per kW.

items.t: Grade & gauge right-size to duty
items.bench: Universal lever
items.note: Thicker lower-grade steel is ~10-25% cheaper per kg and removes unused loss margin.

items.t: Higher sheet utilisation (segmented / nested blanking)
items.bench: Segmented-core motors
items.note: NO-steel skeleton scrap is 35-45% of the coil — recovering it cuts material cost with no design change.

edu.baseline: Premium thin-gauge non-grain-oriented steel, conservative grade, ~60% sheet utilisation.
edu.fn: Conduct flux at low core loss, provide stiffness, be blanked at volume.

edu.name: Rotor magnets / magnet strategy

items.t: Externally-excited (wound-rotor) synchronous motor — delete the magnets
items.bench: BMW Gen6 (rear), ZF/Renault
items.note: Eliminates the volatile heavy-rare-earth magnet bill entirely and de-risks supply — magnets are often a third of motor cost.

items.t: Rare-earth-free permanent-magnet motor (ferrite / non-REE)
items.bench: Tesla next-gen drive unit
items.note: Removes the entire rare-earth cost and supply risk while keeping PM-class controllability.

items.t: Grain-boundary diffusion (GBD) of Dy/Tb
items.bench: Toyota, Hyundai, mainstream IPM
items.note: Heavy rare earth is the costliest, most volatile input — cutting it drops magnet cost 10-25%.

items.t: Rotor oil-cooling to cut heavy rare earth + add ~20% density
items.bench: Audi / Porsche PPE
items.note: Heavy rare earths are the priciest, most volatile magnet ingredient — running cooler lets you specify less.

items.t: Spec-to-duty grade selection (stop over-specifying)
items.bench: Universal lever
items.note: Stops paying for coercivity/temperature headroom the duty never uses.

edu.baseline: Sintered NdFeB IPM, conservative heavy-rare-earth (Dy/Tb) content, full-block magnets.
edu.fn: Provide rotor excitation/torque at temperature without irreversible demagnetisation.

edu.name: Rotor & shaft

items.t: Hollow shaft via flow-forming or friction-welded halves
items.bench: Tesla, BMW, oil-through-rotor designs
items.note: Less material and machining, plus the cooling enables downstream magnet/copper savings.

items.t: Raise speed + gear ratio to shrink active material
items.bench: High-speed e-axles
items.note: Cheapest path to magnet reduction — shrinks the most expensive materials together.

items.t: IPM retention vs surface-PM sleeve (delete sleeve)
items.bench: Most IPM traction motors
items.note: Deletes an expensive sleeve and its assembly.

edu.baseline: Solid gun-drilled shaft, continuous skew, surface-PM retention sleeve, two-plane balance.
edu.fn: Transmit torque, locate the rotor stack/magnets, manage rotordynamics and cooling.

edu.name: Bearings & seals (motor)

items.t: Shaft-current mitigation: grounding ring vs hybrid-ceramic bearing
items.bench: SiC-inverter EVs
items.note: Targets the SiC bearing-current problem at a fraction of the cost of hybrid-ceramic on both ends.

items.t: Bearing size/grade right-size to load & speed
items.bench: Universal lever
items.note: Removes over-specified precision/size.

items.t: Integrated / cassette seal & standardisation
items.bench: Driveline practice
items.note: Cuts piece price via volume and removes an assembly step.

edu.baseline: Two deep-groove ball bearings, contact lip seals, conservative grade; standard steel races.
edu.fn: Support the rotor, exclude contamination, contain oil, manage shaft currents over life.

edu.name: Motor housing

items.t: Large-format HPDC / structural casting to consolidate the housing
items.bench: Tesla, NIO, Zeekr, XPeng (gigacasting)
items.note: Consolidating dozens of parts into one removes joints, sealing faces, fasteners and assembly labour.

items.t: HPDC AlSi10MnMg (Silafont-36) + topology ribs
items.bench: VW MEB, GM Ultium, Tesla
items.note: Cuts aluminium mass 5-12% on the heaviest motor part.

items.t: Integrate cooling jacket as cast-in channels
items.bench: Most modern EDUs
items.note: Deletes a part, seals and assembly; fewer leak points.

edu.baseline: Gravity/standard HPDC aluminium, separate water jacket, machined cooling channels.
edu.fn: Locate stator, carry cooling, provide stiffness/NVH and sealing.

edu.name: Power module

items.t: Proprietary multi-chip SiC module (~75% less SiC die)
items.bench: Tesla next-gen drive unit
items.note: SiC is expensive and hard to scale; cutting die area 60-75% is the biggest single inverter cost lever.

items.t: One inverter platform with selectable Si-or-SiC power stage
items.bench: Stellantis STLA EDM
items.note: Avoids over-specifying SiC on cost trims while amortising one design across millions of units.

items.t: Bring the SiC power module in-house
items.bench: Li Auto, NIO 1200V, BYD
items.note: Removes supplier margin-stacking on the highest-value component and de-risks SiC supply.

items.t: SiC MOSFET module for 800V (1200V class)
items.bench: Tesla, BYD, Hyundai E-GMP, Porsche Taycan
items.note: The efficiency gain downsizes the battery and the faster switching shrinks expensive passives — a system cost-down even though SiC die cost more.

items.t: Die-area sizing to real duty cycle
items.bench: Universal lever
items.note: Semiconductor is the dominant inverter cost; right-sizing the most expensive part pays directly.

edu.baseline: Discrete Si-IGBT module, Al2O3 DBC substrate, Al wire bonds, single-sided cooling, worst-case die sizing.
edu.fn: Switch traction current at required voltage/frequency within thermal-cycle life.

edu.name: Gate driver & control

items.t: Single isolated gate-driver ASIC/SoC
items.bench: Modern traction inverters
items.note: Cuts part count and board area directly.

items.t: PCB layer-count + one board across power classes
items.bench: Platform inverters
items.note: Standardisation + fewer layers cut board cost and SKUs.

items.t: Integrated / coreless current sensing
items.bench: On-module shunt/Hall designs
items.note: Deletes discrete sensor cost and harness.

edu.baseline: Discrete gate drivers + separate digital isolators, multi-layer PCB, discrete current sensors, redundant NTCs.
edu.fn: Drive switches safely/isolated, sense current/voltage/temperature, run motor control.

edu.name: DC-link capacitor

items.t: Downsize via SiC ripple reduction
items.bench: SiC 800V inverters
items.note: The film cap is a top-3 inverter cost; SiC lets you buy less of it.

items.t: Integrate cap + busbar into the power stack
items.bench: Integrated power stacks
items.note: Removes connection hardware and assembly, and the tighter loop allows smaller devices.

edu.baseline: Oversized metallised-PP film capacitor sized for Si-IGBT ripple, separate busbar interface.
edu.fn: Stabilise DC-bus voltage, absorb ripple current, present low loop inductance.

edu.name: Busbar / interconnect

items.t: Aluminium busbar with Cu-clad terminals
items.bench: Cost-down inverters
items.note: Aluminium is far cheaper per kg than copper for the same current at 800V.

items.t: Laser/ultrasonic-welded terminations vs crimp+bolt
items.bench: Modern inverters
items.note: Fewer joints = less labour, lower loss and higher reliability.

items.t: Direct hairpin-terminal → busbar weld
items.bench: Integrated stators
items.note: Removes a connection interface and its parts.

edu.baseline: Solid copper busbars, bolted joints, separate HV connectors.
edu.fn: Carry phase/DC current at low resistance and inductance, interface motor/cap/module.

edu.name: Inverter cold plate / thermal

items.t: FSW or brazed pin-fin cold plate (Al 3003/6061)
items.bench: Mainstream inverters
items.note: Less machining time and scrap.

items.t: Direct-cooled pin-fin baseplate (delete separate cold plate)
items.bench: Integrated power modules
items.note: Deletes the cold plate and a thermal interface, and SiC's lower loss means less cooler needed.

items.t: Avoid AlSiC unless CTE-matching truly required
items.bench: Cost-down practice
items.note: AlSiC is cost-prohibitive; use it only where it is genuinely needed.

edu.baseline: Deep-machined channel cold plate, separate from module, AlSiC where CTE-matched.

edu.name: Gear train

items.t: Lower the reduction ratio (e.g. ~13:1 → 9.8:1) to cut friction
items.bench: VW APP550

items.t: Near-net / precision-forged blanks

items.t: Isotropic superfinish on high-duty flanks
items.bench: Premium/efficiency-led EVs
items.note: Pays back through efficiency, lower oil viscosity and reduced cooling.

edu.name: Gearbox / transmission housing

items.t: Cast-in oil galleries & sensor bosses
items.bench: Modern gear housings
items.note: Deletes drilling, plugs and leak paths.

items.t: Tolerance right-size (IT6/IT7 only on bearing seats)

items.t: Magnesium covers (AZ91D / AM60B) where non-pressure

edu.name: Park lock mechanism

edu.name: Differential & output shafts

items.t: Net-shape forged / PM bevel & side gears
items.bench: Driveline practice
items.note: Removes most machining on these gears.

items.t: Welded ring gear to diff case (delete bolts)

items.note: Removes the bolt circle and machining.

edu.name: Thermal management / cooling circuit

items.t: Delete the electric oil pump — passive gear-driven splash + coolant-coupled oil
items.bench: VW APP550
items.note: Deletes a bought component and its parasitic electrical load, improving both cost and efficiency.

items.t: Single-fluid (oil) cooling + lubrication
items.bench: Ford F-150 Lightning, modern e-axles
items.note: Removes a whole circuit, its seals and leak points — 20-35% of the thermal subsystem.

items.t: Direct rotor / end-turn oil cooling
items.bench: Tesla, premium e-axles
items.note: Better cooling unlocks downstream active-material savings.

edu.baseline: Separate water-glycol jacket for motor+inverter plus an oil circuit for gears; two pumps, external oil cooler.
edu.fn: Keep magnets, windings, dies and gears within temperature at all duty points.

edu.name: Lubrication system

items.t: Lifetime fill at reduced volume + baffled sump

items.note: Less oil cost and lower drag loss.

items.t: Low-viscosity ATF enabled by superfinished gears
items.bench: Premium driveline
items.note: Efficiency gain compounds with 800V range/cost targets.

items.bench: Hyundai E-GMP, Tesla, BYD 8-in-1

edu.name: Position & current sensors

items.t: Resolver → inductive (eddy-current) / TMR-GMR sensor

edu.name: EDU integration / architecture

items.t: 8-in-1 e-axle: motor+gear+inverter+OBC+DC-DC+BMS+VCU+MCU in one box
items.bench: BYD e-Platform 3.0 (Seal)
items.note: Every function folded in deletes a housing, a connector set and a wiring run, plus assembly stations.

items.t: Scalable-length motor family on one shared inverter platform
items.bench: Mercedes eATS 2.0, Audi PPE, XPeng SEPA2.0

items.bench: BYD 8-in-1 (Seal), Tesla, Geely

items.bench: Ford F-150 Lightning (shared front/rear)

items.t: 48V low-voltage net to cut LV copper
items.bench: Tesla Cybertruck

edu.name: Axial-Flux Motor Integration

items.bench: Mercedes AMG SL 53 (YASA), Ferrari SF90 Stradale, Stellantis (YASA acquisition 2021)
items.note: Halves active copper, iron and magnet mass for equal peak torque at matched speed; critical for unsprung weight targets in in-wheel or hub-close configurations.

items.t: Magnax axial-flux (no rotor back-iron): 15 kW/kg — eliminate rotor iron
items.bench: Magnax MX-45 (Belgium) — 225 kW, 15 kW/kg at 15k rpm; adopted by aerospace/industrial
items.note: Eliminating rotor iron (40-50% of rotor mass) and concentrating flux reduces magnet content while raising power density to aerospace-class.

items.bench: Printed Motor Works (UK, Valeo partnership), Saietta (Agility), light-duty applications
items.note: Complete elimination of copper winding labour and insulation process; enables automated PCB production of motor stators.

edu.fn: Deliver high power density with reduced active-material mass by exploiting axial-flux topology.

edu.name: EESM — Electrically Excited Synchronous Motor (Wound Rotor)

edu.name: Multi-Speed EV Gearbox (2-Speed & Above)

items.bench: Porsche Taycan 2-speed (PDK rear), Rimac Nevera 2-speed per axle, Stellantis STLA 2-speed

items.note: Every metre of wire removed saves material and weight; fewer splices and connectors reduce failure modes and warranty cost. Zone architecture also enables OTA updates to replace costly dealer visits.

items.note: Eliminating dealer reflash events saves £68–128 per vehicle per update cycle, compounds over the fleet, and monetises software updates as revenue.

items.bench: Tesla's octopus architecture (Model 3/Y); Volkswagen SSP Zonal (2026); Toyota E3 zonal (20
items.note: Zonal architecture eliminates the long cross-car wiring runs that are most expensive to route, test and service. Central compute enables software-defined features sold post-sale.

items.note: DC-DC converter is typically £68–128 BOM; 48V LV harness is 25% lighter. For BEV, where 12V battery serves only accessories, the case for a separate 12V subsystem is purely inertia.

ee.baseline: Distributed ECU architecture: 70–150 discrete ECUs, each function in a separate control unit, CAN bus backbone, proprietary middleware per supplier, dealer-reflash for software updates.
ee.fn: Execute all vehicle functions in software — safety, powertrain, chassis, body, ADAS, infotainment — with deterministic real-time performance, functional safety, and OTA software update capability.
